data.title: HYPE Experiences Volatility Amid Market Movements
data.description:
On April 22, HYPE's price fluctuated around $40 following a market upswing earlier in the day, with a 24-hour decline of 3%. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, a significant trader on Hyperliquid, identified by the address 0xec8, opened a short position with 5x leverage on HYPE within the past hour, averaging $40.04 per unit and facing a liquidation price of $46.6. The position's size is approximately $3.38 million.

Additionally, another major trader, with a position exceeding $1 million, is closest to the liquidation line. This trader, identified by the address 0x4a8, opened their position at 1 a.m. today with an average price of $39.64. The position has shifted from profit to loss, and if HYPE's price rises to $41.6, it will trigger liquidation.

Address: 0xdf86c7c9069e9bd5bac9d97e21f9aa10f728fb86

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Lead Crypto Futures Liquidations
data.description: Bitcoin and Ethereum have emerged as the leading assets in 24-hour crypto futures liquidations, with Bitcoin seeing $160 million and Ethereum $92.31 million in liquidations. According to NS3.AI, the majority of these liquidations were short positions in both cryptocurrencies.

data.title: Ripple and Unloq Test Automated Trade Settlements with RLUSD in Singapore
data.description: Ripple and Unloq are collaborating to explore automated trade settlements using Ripple's forthcoming stablecoin, RLUSD, on the XRP Ledger. According to NS3.AI, this initiative is part of Singapore's Project BLOOM sandbox pilot, which aims to assess the integration of digital settlement assets into the current financial infrastructure. RLUSD is currently in a sandbox testing phase and has not yet received official licensure or a comprehensive regulatory mandate.

data.title: Ethereum Spot ETFs See Nine-Day Inflow Streak
data.description: Ethereum spot ETFs recorded a net inflow of $43.3589 million yesterday, marking the ninth consecutive day of positive inflows. According to NS3.AI, BlackRock's ETHA was the leading contributor with $37.0017 million in inflows. In contrast, Grayscale's ETHE experienced the largest outflow, amounting to $12.1355 million.

data.title: UK Startup Stratiphy Introduces Crypto ETNs and Innovative Financial ISA
data.description:
UK startup Stratiphy is set to offer cryptocurrency exchange-traded notes (ETNs) alongside an innovative financial ISA (IF ISA), allowing investors to hold crypto assets within tax-free accounts. According to Foresight News, Stratiphy will provide three ETNs issued by 21Shares, Europe's largest crypto ETP issuer, tracking Bitcoin, Ethereum, and a Bitcoin-Gold combination product. The platform currently manages approximately £4 million in assets, serving around 2,000 clients.

In October last year, the UK's Financial Conduct Authority lifted a four-year ban on retail investors purchasing exchange-traded notes (ETNs).

data.title: Tesla to launch AI voice function
data.description: According to Odaily Planet Daily, the Shanghai Cyberspace Administration has added one generative AI service that has completed its registration, providing a large-scale voice model service for Tesla's in-vehicle system. In response, a Tesla representative stated on April 22nd, "Stay tuned." (Yicai)

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $43.3589 million yesterday, marking the ninth consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (April 21, Eastern Time), Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $43.3589 million. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a net inflow of $37.0017 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $11.943 billion. This was followed by BlackRock Stalled ETH ETF ETHB, with a net inflow of $15.4593 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $424 million. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was Grayscale Ethereum Trust ETF ETHE, with a net outflow of $12.1355 million, bringing its historical total net outflow to $5.227 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of the Ethereum spot ETF is $13.665 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) is 4.89%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $12.055 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $11.8442 million yesterday, marking the sixth consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $11.8442 million yesterday (April 21, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was BlackRock ETF IBIT, with a net inflow of $39.3447 million, bringing IBIT's historical total net inflow to $64.928 billion. This was followed by Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF BTC, with a net inflow of $17.259 million, bringing BTC's historical total net inflow to $2.274 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was Grayscale ETF GBTC, with a net outflow of $17.5094 million, bringing GBTC's historical total net outflow to $26.199 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $99.081 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.54%, and a cumulative net inflow of $57.99 billion.

data.title: BitMart contracts will be listed on NATUSDT.
data.description: According to the official announcement, BitMart will launch NATUSDT perpetual contracts on April 22, 2026 at 15:00 (UTC+8), supporting leverage up to 20x.

data.title: Trump retweeted the post late at night, claiming that he had been preparing for war with Iran for over 40 years.
data.description: Late last night local time, US President Trump retweeted a post by American activist Brigitte Gabriel, which read: "President Trump has been preparing for this war with Iran for over 40 years!" Trump also retweeted a video of an interview with US counterterrorism expert Jim Hanson, in which he stated that the US has completely won this war. (Jinshi)

data.title: X Platform Introduces Snooze Topics Feature for Premium Users
data.description: X product lead Nikita Bier announced the launch of Snooze Topics on the platform. According to Odaily, this feature allows Premium subscribers to hide selected topics for 24 hours on their For You homepage. Users can choose from topics such as politics, video, sports, business and finance, science and technology, entertainment and arts, and artificial intelligence. The Reset option enables users to remove the snooze at any time. This feature is available on both iOS and web platforms and was launched alongside Custom Timelines.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Comments on Iran's Financial Crisis
data.description: On April 22, U.S. President Donald Trump shared on social media that Iran is facing a severe financial crisis. According to BlockBeats, Trump stated that Iran urgently needs to open the Strait of Hormuz due to extreme financial shortages, with daily losses amounting to $500 million. Additionally, military and police personnel are reportedly complaining about delayed salaries.

data.title: Trump: Iran's finances are collapsing
data.description: US President Trump stated that Iran's finances are collapsing and they desperately need cash—they are losing $500 million daily. Iranian military and police are also complaining about not receiving their salaries. (Jinshi)

data.title: Justin Sun Files Lawsuit Against World Liberty Financial to Protect Token Holder Rights
data.description: Justin Sun announced on the X platform that he has filed a lawsuit in a California federal court against World Liberty Financial to safeguard his rights as a $WLFI token holder. According to Odaily, Sun emphasized his longstanding support for U.S. President Donald Trump and his administration's efforts to make the United States a crypto-friendly nation. He stated that the lawsuit does not alter his views on Trump or his administration. Sun expressed disappointment that certain individuals within the World Liberty project team have operated in ways that contradict Trump's values. These individuals allegedly froze all of Sun's tokens, deprived him of voting rights on governance proposals, and threatened to permanently destroy his tokens through "burning," all without justification. Sun believes that if Trump were aware of these actions, he would not tolerate them.

data.title: X Product Manager Nikita Bier announced the launch of Snooze Topics
data.description: Nikita Bier, product manager for X, announced the launch of Snooze Topics on the platform. Premium subscribers can hide selected topics for 24 hours on the For You homepage. Available topics include politics, video, sports, business and finance, science and technology, entertainment and art, and artificial intelligence. These topics can be unhide at any time by clicking "Reset." The feature is available on both iOS and web platforms, launching alongside Custom Timelines on the same day.

data.title: Significant Capital Outflows from Aave Amid Ongoing Financial Shifts
data.description:
Blockchain sleuth EmberCN posted on X that Aave has experienced substantial capital outflows over the past three and a half days, with approximately $15.1 billion being withdrawn. The total deposits have decreased from $48.5 billion before the incident to $30.7 billion, marking a withdrawal of about one-third of the funds.

In comparison, other major lending platforms have shown varied trends. Morpho's total deposits have declined from $11.7 billion before the rsETH incident to $10.2 billion, resulting in a $1.5 billion outflow. Conversely, Spark's SparkLend business has seen an increase in total value locked (TVL), rising from $1.9 billion before the rsETH incident to $3.2 billion, indicating an inflow of $1.3 billion.

Spark has managed to attract some large investors and institutions, including Justin Sun and a whale who invested $500 million in February, redirecting funds withdrawn from Aave.

data.title: Whale Sells ASTEROID Tokens for Significant Profit
data.description: A whale has reportedly sold 2.79 billion ASTEROID tokens, according to Odaily. The transaction resulted in an exchange for 503 ETH, valued at $1.17 million. This sale comes just five days after the whale purchased the tokens for only $575, achieving a return of over 2000 times the initial investment.

data.title: A whale (a cryptocurrency investor) posted a screenshot showing its holdings of 717.491 BTC, with a floating profit of $1.226 million, after setting "10 major goals" in advance.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, a whale who posted a screenshot showing a position of 717.491 BTC with a floating profit of $1.226 million, opened at $75,731.7 and is currently trading at $77,439.7.

data.title: A whale spent 1.9 ETH to buy 44.03 billion FLORK in 2023, which is now worth over $350,000.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale spent 1.9 ETH (US$3,941) to buy 44.03 billion FLORK on April 18, 2023, which is now worth more than US$350,000. However, the whale's wallet address has not been active for 976 days.

data.title: Japanese Yen Faces Limited Movement Amid Economic Concerns
data.description: The Japanese yen remains confined within the 158.00-160.00 range against the U.S. dollar, according to Jin10. The potential for Japanese intervention in the foreign exchange market is curbing upward movement, while uncertainties in the Middle East, high energy prices, and demand for the dollar as a safe haven are limiting downward movement. A significant rebound in the yen could occur only if the Bank of Japan raises interest rates, potentially pushing it to around 155 or stronger. If the Bank of Japan unexpectedly acts during its policy meeting on April 27-28, it could significantly suppress the dollar-yen exchange rate, possibly causing a decline before Japan's upcoming 'Golden Week' holiday. However, this scenario seems unlikely at present. Data from Totan Research/ICAP indicates that the probability of a rate hike next week has decreased to 9%, while the likelihood of a June hike has risen to 62%. Despite concerns from the Bank of Japan and the Ministry of Finance about the inflationary impact of a weak yen, the market perceives that Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda and most policy committee members remain cautious about the economic impact of the Middle East situation amid heightened worries about economic growth setbacks.
